canonical_definition: Across the provided sources, WikiSQL is consistently characterized as a benchmark dataset for text-to-SQL tasks. The prevailing definition describes it as a "standard text-to-SQL dataset," with one paper specifying its focus on parsing over single relational tables. A less common framing characterizes it as a benchmark for evaluating model performance on "structured knowledge grounding tasks." The primary use of WikiSQL is for evaluation, as it is shown in tables comparing model results against baselines. In terms of what it assesses, one paper reports that WikiSQL is used to measure the construct of "Table understanding." The dataset is also mentioned as a corpus to which new methodologies can be adapted and is situated alongside other benchmarks like Spider.
papers: SEMMA: A Semantic Aware Knowledge Graph Foundation Model
Training a Utility-based Retriever Through Shared Context Attribution for Retrieval-Augmented Language Models
DAWN-ICL: Strategic Planning of Problem-solving Trajectories for Zero-Shot In-Context Learning
TheRussian-focused embedders’ exploration: ruMTEBbenchmark andRussian embedding model design

definitions.text: WikiSQL is a standard text-to-SQL dataset referenced as another corpus the framework could be adapted to.
definitions.paper: DAWN-ICL: Strategic Planning of Problem-solving Trajectories for Zero-Shot In-Context Learning
